okay so changes to horla pv translation <@289320818901778434> 
most of them are minor and clarifications of certain wordings, but one that's really interesting is how it changes from "a poem dedicated to the garden" to "an offering to the garden", because while yeah the new tl (upper) is more technically accurate as an offering of spring, the word "offering" suggests Horla sort of worships/gives tribute to Colette, verses the "dedication" suggesting she did it for herself, giving it *to* Colette as a representation of their relationship.
other change that makes me go "oooh" is "you shall __build__ me a garden" and "you will __give__ me a garden" with the initial tl (give) making me initially think colette simply already *had* that garden, and was gifting Horla a place of respite, but "build" is actually more accurate to the context, where Colette uses what she has to secure and create for the two of them their own little world in the garden. 
then going on, there's the increase of religious/worshipful imagery in their relationship again, w/ the words "pray" and "offering" being used, when Horla writes the poem, rather than the initial tl of "springtime gift"
> I think it might be intentional at this point, to emphasize how unequal their relationship was, but I'm just not a super big fan of that wording?
Then there's the ending, with "When we meet again..." splitting off into "shall you bring me flowers/will you bring a bouquet of flowers", and honestly I prefer the old TL in that section better, just because.
We also have the text change from "Bouquet for Spring" to "A final Bouquet for Spring...", which considering it's meant to be the text in French on-screen, I prefer, again, the old tl in that part
We also have the change from "verse" used throughout the pv to "stanza", which I feel kinda breaks meaning and loses the connection to Horla's wheel that I felt was there at the end of the old pv, since it was "the final verse", and not "the last stanza"
overall I do like the new tl though, it adds nice context to most of the scenes, but some of the new wording is interesting

Questions I was fortunately able to get answered, given that their Studio is fairly friendly. So, join me as I find out more a
